mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following payroll growth do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month acceleration in the federal funds rate exceeds the market's pricing of future policy tightening, triggering a repricing of discount rates and equity valuations that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and residential mortgage debt outstanding indicates synchronized credit expansion and consumer confidence, which amplifies corporate earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns in at least 55% of observations when both series rise simultaneously, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals the normalization of supply chains and capacity utilization, which reduces input cost volatility and supports equity multiples, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during the specified recovery window, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing below the 4.0% threshold signals a tight labor market that historically precedes wage-driven consumption growth and earnings upgrades, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not statistically higher than baseline when UNRATE breaches the 4.0% threshold downward, or the signal generates negative forward returns in > 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding corporate sales and profit margins, which mechanically supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in core inflation and rapid payroll growth forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than priced in, compressing equity risk appetite and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both CPI and PAYEMS accelerate concurrently, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarter-over-quarter contraction in housing starts signals deteriorating consumer credit conditions and business investment sentiment, which transmits to broader equity markets and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST contracts by more than 5% quarter-over-quarter, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when adjusted for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.
